human_review_reason: Source URL missing prevents verification of link integrity. Content is substantively accurate and well-sourced from authoritative NY Fed documentation, but metadata gaps require human confirmation that this is the correct canonical page before acceptance.

corrected_fields.macro_context: Repo and reverse repo operations are part of the Federal Reserve's standard monetary policy toolkit, conducted daily to maintain the fed funds rate within the FOMC's target range. ON RRP and standing repo serve as the lower and upper bounds of the interest rate corridor, respectively.
corrected_fields.market_structure_context: The ON RRP and standing repo facilities form the boundaries of the Fed's interest rate corridor system, directly shaping repo market dynamics, short-term funding costs, and money market fund flows. This structure is fundamental to understanding how the Fed implements monetary policy through money market operations.
